question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

question: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be István Kapitány?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's commanding 99.9% trader consensus as Hungary's next prime minister stems from his Tisza party's landslide victory in the April 12, 2026, parliamentary elections, securing a two-thirds supermajority with 141 of 199 National Assembly seats amid record turnout. Viktor Orbán conceded defeat shortly after, ending Fidesz's 16-year dominance and confirming Magyar—a former ruling party insider turned center-right challenger—as the clear leader positioned to form the government. This outcome reflects widespread voter rejection of Orbán's nationalist rule in favor of Tisza's pro-EU platform. The market awaits formal investiture, likely by May 9 when the new parliament convenes; only an extraordinary legal challenge, parliamentary defection, or unforeseen health event could realistically disrupt this path, though such barriers remain negligible given the margin.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid Pakistan-mediated diplomacy following the April 7 ceasefire in the 2026 US-Iran war—initiated by late February US-Israeli strikes on Iranian military sites—trader consensus favors a permanent peace deal by year-end, reflecting Iran's ongoing review of a US one-page memorandum proposal. This framework proposes a nuclear enrichment moratorium, partial sanctions relief, and Strait of Hormuz reopening in exchange for pausing US operations like Project Freedom. Persistent gaps on ballistic missiles, frozen assets, and regional proxies including Hezbollah in Lebanon underscore barriers to a qualifying permanent accord, defined as a signed treaty or mutual confirmation explicitly ending hostilities indefinitely. Further Islamabad talks could advance de-escalation or highlight impasses.

question: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads trader consensus at 17% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, narrowly ahead of Spain following Lamine Yamal's hamstring injury that ended his Barcelona season but with expected recovery in time for the June 11 opener. This tight race among top contenders—Spain (15%), England (11%), and Argentina/Brazil (9% each)—stems from balanced squads post-group draw, where no team dominates despite Spain's Euro 2024 title, France's 2022 final pedigree, England's deep tournament runs, and South American powers' historical knockout edge. Recent injury trackers highlight concerns like England's Jack Grealish stress fracture and Brazil's Rodrygo ACL doubts, while preliminary squad lists due May 11 add uncertainty to form amid club season finales and sparse friendlies. The 48-team expansion amplifies upset potential, keeping probabilities clustered.

question: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Federico Cina vs Alexander Blockx
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Federico Cina and Alexander Blockx in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Federico Cina' if Federico Cina advances against Alexander Blockx.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Blockx' if Alexander Blockx advances against Federico Cina.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexander Blockx enters the Internazionali BNL d'Italia as the trader consensus favorite, buoyed by his career-high No. 36 ATP ranking and red-hot clay form, including a round-of-16 breakthrough at Madrid Masters where the 21-year-old Belgian upset world No. 5 Felix Auger-Aliassime 7-6(3), 6-3 before falling to Francisco Cerundolo. Italian wild card Federico Cina, a 19-year-old ranked No. 225 with a peak of 183, draws home-crowd energy at Foro Italico but arrives off a qualifying loss to Juan Manuel Cerundolo in Madrid and limited Masters experience. No head-to-head history exists between the right-handers; Blockx's top-25 Elo rating, recent deep Masters runs, and stylistic edge on clay shape the closely contested round-of-128 matchup, though Cina's youth and surface familiarity add upset potential.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 15, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 15, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 15, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing military tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, stemming from the US-Israel-Iran conflict that erupted in late February 2026, continue to suppress commercial shipping to under 10% of pre-war levels, with only 7-9 vessels transiting daily versus the normal 100-140. Recent attacks on two cargo ships reported on May 4 by the UK Maritime Trade Operations Centre, coupled with US-led naval blockades on Iranian ports and Iran's reversal of brief openings, have reinforced trader consensus on prolonged disruption. Live tracking data shows no surge in traffic, and with no diplomatic breakthroughs or ceasefire signals in the past week, the 93% implied probability for "No" reflects significant barriers to normalization by May 15 amid persistent escalation risks and stranded vessels exceeding 1,500.

question: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Roman Andres Burruchaga vs Mattia Bellucci
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Roman Andres Burruchaga and Mattia Bellucci in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Roman Andres Burruchaga' if Roman Andres Burruchaga advances against Mattia Bellucci.

This market will resolve to 'Mattia Bellucci' if Mattia Bellucci advances against Roman Andres Burruchaga.

eventMetadata.context_description: Roman Andres Burruchaga enters this Internazionali BNL d'Italia first-round matchup on outdoor clay as the higher-ranked player at No. 56, riding strong 2026 clay form with a 17-6 record highlighted by a Houston ATP 250 final loss to Tommy Paul last month and recent Cagliari Challenger wins over Pellegrino and Giron. Mattia Bellucci, ranked No. 80 with a 5-5 clay mark this year, gains home-crowd boost at Foro Italico as a likely wildcard, fresh off upsetting higher-ranked Lorenzo Sonego in Cagliari but inconsistent lately. No head-to-head exists; Burruchaga's superior movement and rally endurance contrast Bellucci's power game, with no reported injuries shaping trader consensus on this best-of-three encounter.

question: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Team WE (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Team WE in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 7 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Team WE.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Weibo Gaming enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 as the trader-favored side, buoyed by a 2-0 sweep over Team WE on April 22 and an overall 2-6 series record that outpaces WE's winless 0-7 mark at the Group Ascend bottom. WBG's recent 0-2 loss to Top Esports on May 2 exposed botlane inconsistencies under Elk and Hang, but Jiejie's jungle pressure and Xiaohu's midlane stability remain strengths against WE's faltering macro, evident in their 1-2 defeat to JDG last week. With top-four direct playoff qualification on the line, expect focus on draft edges in the current patch favoring aggressive early rotates; both squads boast stable rosters absent injury concerns.

question: LoL: HANJIN BRION vs BNK FEARX - Game 2 Winner
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between HANJIN BRION and BNK FEARX in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 7 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "HANJIN BRION" if HANJIN BRION win Game 2 against BNK FEARX.

This market will resolve to "BNK FEARX" if BNK FEARX win Game 2 against HANJIN BRION.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 2 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 2. 

If Game 2 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: HANJIN BRION sits 6th in LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2 standings with a 4-7 series record, facing 9th-placed BNK FEARX (3-8) in this Week 6 BO3 rematch after sweeping them 2-0 in Week 1. BRION's rebuilt roster, anchored by veteran ADC Teddy alongside top laner Casting and jungler GIDEON, has shown flashes against bottom-half teams despite losses to top contenders like KT and T1. FEARX struggles with inconsistency and recent bot lane roster flux, installing Taeyoon after cycling Slayer and Diable, limiting their upset potential in the double round-robin format where mid-table positioning aids Road to MSI qualification. Head-to-head dominance and superior games differential favor BRION's trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: HANJIN BRION sits 6th in LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2 standings with a 4-7 series record, facing 9th-placed BNK FEARX (3-8) in this Week 6 BO3 rematch after sweeping them 2-0 in Week 1. BRION's rebuilt roster, anchored by veteran ADC Teddy alongside top laner Casting and jungler GIDEON, has shown flashes against bottom-half teams despite losses to top contenders like KT and T1. FEARX struggles with inconsistency and recent bot lane roster flux, installing Taeyoon after cycling Slayer and Diable, limiting their upset potential in the double round-robin format where mid-table positioning aids Road to MSI qualification. Head-to-head dominance and superior games differential favor BRION's trader consensus.

question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 80.5% implied probability against a U.S. ground invasion of Iran before 2027, driven by accelerating diplomatic breakthroughs amid the ongoing 2026 conflict. Following U.S. and Israeli airstrikes since late February targeting Iranian military sites and Kharg Island, recent de-escalation signals dominate: President Trump paused "Project Freedom" naval operations in the Strait of Hormuz last week after announcing progress in indirect talks mediated by Pakistan, while Iran conveyed a formal response to the U.S. 15-point proposal yesterday, deeming it one-sided yet keeping negotiations open with a three-stage de-escalation plan. Iran is expected to reply to the latest U.S. memorandum today, fostering hopes for a ceasefire deal averting escalation to boots-on-the-ground invasion, consistent with historical aversion to large-scale Middle East ground wars. Fragile truce holds as both sides prioritize talks over further military action.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid Pakistan-mediated diplomacy following the April 7 ceasefire in the 2026 US-Iran war—initiated by late February US-Israeli strikes on Iranian military sites—trader consensus favors a permanent peace deal by year-end, reflecting Iran's ongoing review of a US one-page memorandum proposal. This framework proposes a nuclear enrichment moratorium, partial sanctions relief, and Strait of Hormuz reopening in exchange for pausing US operations like Project Freedom. Persistent gaps on ballistic missiles, frozen assets, and regional proxies including Hezbollah in Lebanon underscore barriers to a qualifying permanent accord, defined as a signed treaty or mutual confirmation explicitly ending hostilities indefinitely. Further Islamabad talks could advance de-escalation or highlight impasses.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Iranian regime fall by June 30, with "No" implying 95.5% probability, driven by the regime's intensified crackdown on mass protests sparked by April US-Israel airstrikes and naval blockade. Over 53,000 arrests, dozens of executions, nationwide internet blackouts, and pro-regime rallies have quashed unrest, while officials like parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf assert unity and warn aggressors. Economic strain from $500 billion in damages and halted oil exports persists, but Supreme Leader Khamenei's defiant statements signal cohesion among security forces including the IRGC. Realistic shifts could involve elite defections, uncontainable nationwide uprisings, or leadership vacuum from health events, though historical resilience against prior protests bolsters high confidence in stability.

question: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Anyone's Legend - Game 2 Winner
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 7 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win Game 2 against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win Game 2 against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 2 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 2. 

If Game 2 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 7 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch, buoyed by their 7-3 record atop the standings versus Ninjas in Pyjamas' middling 4-5 mark and a decisive 2-0 head-to-head win on April 22 where jungler Tarzan earned MVP honors in both games. AL's stable roster—Flandre top, Tarzan jungle, Shanks mid, Hope ADC, Kael support—has delivered consistent macro execution in the double round-robin format, climbing to second place with strong early-game pressure. NIP's revamped lineup featuring Korean imports Hoya top and Guwon jungle shows upset potential after sweeping JD Gaming, but recent losses like 0-2 to Top Esports highlight adaptation struggles ahead of playoffs qualification battles.
